description: Session 17 — Fix notification dot, type push events, register macOS notification categories with action buttons

userStories.title: Fix notification dot clipping on bubble
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a red notification dot on the bubble when the bot responds while the chat is closed, so I know there's a new message.
acceptanceCriteria: Notification dot (14pt, #c25a4a) is fully visible at top-right of the 64pt bubble circle
Wrapper NSView frame is large enough to contain both the bubble circle and the dot without clipping
Dot has 2pt dark border (#28241f) for contrast against any background
DragView covers the full wrapper area so drag still works with the larger panel
Panel frame origin calculation accounts for the extra padding (uses panelSize, not bubbleSize for screen edge math)
showNotificationDot() fades in (0.2s), hideNotificationDot() fades out (0.15s)
Dot shows when stream finishes with chat closed, and when push message arrives with chat closed
Dot hides when showChat() is called
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild builds without errors)
userStories.notes: File: ErestorApp/Services/BubbleWindowController.swift. The wrapper approach is already implemented but may have frame math issues. Verify that NSRect(x: bubbleSize - dotSize/2, y: bubbleSize - dotSize/2, width: dotSize, height: dotSize) positions correctly within the wrapper NSRect(x: 0, y: 0, width: bubbleSize + padding, height: bubbleSize + padding). Build command: cd ~/projetos/erestor/ErestorApp && xcodegen generate && xcodebuild -project ErestorApp.xcodeproj -scheme ErestorApp -configuration Debug -derivedDataPath build/DerivedData build

userStories.title: Type push events in Python backend — add poll_energy, poll_quality, gate_inform, reminder
userStories.description: As the system, I need typed push events so the desktop app can render different UI (poll cards, alert cards, reminders) instead of treating everything as a plain message.
acceptanceCriteria: gate.py _deliver_to_desktop() accepts an event_type parameter (default 'message' for backward compat)
New push event types supported: 'poll_energy', 'poll_quality', 'gate_inform', 'reminder'
poll_energy payload: {type: 'poll_energy', text: prompt_text, options: ['1-morto','2-baixa','3-ok','4-boa','5-pico']}
poll_quality payload: {type: 'poll_quality', text: prompt_text, options: ['perdi','meh','ok','flow']}
gate_inform payload: {type: 'gate_inform', text: alert_text, severity: 'amber'|'red', actions: [...]}
reminder payload: {type: 'reminder', text: reminder_text}
Existing 'message' type push events continue to work unchanged
Python syntax check passes (python3 -m py_compile erestor/gate.py)
userStories.notes: File: ~/claude-sync/produtividade/erestor/gate.py (function _deliver_to_desktop around line 548) and ~/claude-sync/produtividade/erestor_api.py (push_event/drain_push_events around line 212). IMPORTANT: This story runs in ~/claude-sync/produtividade/, NOT in the ErestorApp directory. The push events are delivered via HTTP POST to /api/push/deliver and polled by the app via GET /api/push/pending.

userStories.title: Handle typed push events in Swift app
userStories.description: As the app, I need to handle poll_energy, poll_quality, gate_inform, and reminder push events differently from plain messages, so the UI can render appropriate cards.
acceptanceCriteria: PushEvent model in Message.swift has typed enum or string for type field covering: message, poll_energy, poll_quality, gate_inform, reminder
PushEvent includes optional fields: options ([String]), severity (String), actions ([ChatAction])

ChatService.handlePushEvent() routes each type correctly: message → existing behavior, poll_energy/poll_quality → post notification with type info, gate_inform → post notification with severity, reminder → post notification
NotificationCenter userInfo includes event type and payload so BubbleWindowController can differentiate
BubbleWindowController.sendPushNotification() uses event type to set notification title (e.g. 'Erestor — Enquete' for polls, 'Erestor — Alerta' for gate_inform)
All existing message-type push events continue to work unchanged
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild builds without errors)
userStories.notes: Files: ErestorApp/Models/Message.swift (PushEvent struct), ErestorApp/Services/ChatService.swift (handlePushEvent), ErestorApp/Services/BubbleWindowController.swift (sendPushNotification, observePushMessages). Depends on US-002 for backend payload format.

userStories.title: Register macOS notification categories with action buttons
userStories.description: As a user, I want notification buttons so I can respond to polls and alerts directly from the macOS notification banner without opening the chat.
acceptanceCriteria: UNNotificationCategory 'POLL_ENERGY' registered with 4 actions: '1-morto', '2-baixa', '3-ok', '4-boa' (max 4 buttons, 5th option opens panel)
UNNotificationCategory 'POLL_QUALITY' registered with 4 actions: 'perdi', 'meh', 'ok', 'flow'
UNNotificationCategory 'GATE_INFORM' registered with 2 actions: 'Ver' (opens chat) and 'Dispensar' (dismisses)
UNNotificationCategory 'REMINDER' registered with 2 actions: 'Ver' and 'Dispensar'
Categories registered in AppDelegate.applicationDidFinishLaunching via UNUserNotificationCenter.current().setNotificationCategories()
sendPushNotification sets content.categoryIdentifier based on push event type
didReceive response handler reads actionIdentifier and sends response back to backend via POST /api/push/respond
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild builds without errors)
userStories.notes: Files: ErestorApp/ErestorApp.swift (AppDelegate — register categories in launch, handle didReceive), ErestorApp/Services/BubbleWindowController.swift (set categoryIdentifier in sendPushNotification). Note: macOS notification actions are limited to 4 buttons max. Energy poll has 5 options so the '5-pico' option should open the panel instead. The /api/push/respond endpoint does not exist yet — it needs to be created in erestor_api.py (backend, separate repo).
